text.NRSV: When Saul had taken the kingship over Israel, he fought against all his enemies on every side—against Moab, against the Ammonites, against Edom, against the kings of Zobah, and against the Philistines; wherever he turned he was victorious.
text.Rotherham: And, Saul, took possession of the kingdom over Israel,—and made war round about against all his enemies—against Moab, and against the sons of Ammon, and against Edom, and against the kings of Zobah, and against the Philistines, and, whomsoever he turned against, he was victorious.
text.KJV: 14:47 ¶ So Saul tooke the kingdom ouer Israel, and fought against all his enemies on euery side, against Moab, and against the children of Ammon, and against Edom, and against the kings of Zobah, and against the Philistines: and whithersoeuer hee turned himselfe, he vexed them.
text.NABRE: 14:47 Saul’s Victories. After taking possession of the kingship over Israel, Saul waged war on its enemies all around—Moab, the Ammonites, Edom, the kings of Zobah, and the Philistines. Wherever he turned, he was successful

text.NRSV: He did valiantly, and struck down the Amalekites, and rescued Israel out of the hands of those who plundered them.
text.Rotherham: Then made he ready a force, and smote the Amalekites,—and delivered Israel out of the hand of such as plundered them.
text.KJV: 14:48 And he gathered an hoste, and smote the Amalekites, and deliuered Israel out of the handes of them that spoiled them.
text.NABRE: 14:48 and fought bravely. He defeated Amalek and delivered Israel from the hand of those who were plundering them.

text.NRSV: The sons of Saul were Jonathan, Ishvi, and Malchishua; and the names of his two daughters were these: the name of the firstborn was Merab, and the name of the younger, Michal.
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Saul were Jonathan, and Ishvi, and Malchishua,—and, the names of his two daughters, were, the name of the firstborn, Merab, and, the name of the younger, Michal,—
text.KJV: 14:49 Now the sonnes of Saul, were Ionathan, and Ishui, and Melchishua: and the names of his two daughters were these: the name of the first borne Merab, and the name of the yonger Michal:
text.NABRE: 14:49 Saul’s Family. The sons of Saul were Jonathan, Ishvi, and Malchishua; the name of his firstborn daughter was Merob; the name of the younger was Michal.

text.NRSV: The name of Saul’s wife was Ahinoam daughter of Ahimaaz. And the name of the commander of his army was Abner son of Ner, Saul’s uncle;
text.Rotherham: and, the name of Saul’s wife, was Ahinoam, daughter of Ahimaaz,—and the name of the prince of his host, Abner, son of Ner, Saul’s uncle.
text.KJV: 14:50 And the name of Sauls wife was Ahinoam, the daughter of Ahimaaz, and the name of the captaine of his host was Abner, the sonne of Ner, Sauls vncle.
text.NABRE: 14:50 The name of Saul’s wife was Ahinoam, daughter of Ahimaaz. The name of his general was Abner, son of Ner, Saul’s uncle;

text.NRSV: Kish was the father of Saul, and Ner father of Abner was the son of Abiel.
text.Rotherham: And, Kish, was Saul’s father, and, Ner, Abner’s father, was son of Abiel.
text.KJV: 14:51 And Kish was the father of Saul, and Ner the father of Abner was the sonne of Abiel.
text.NABRE: 14:51 Kish, Saul’s father, and Ner, Abner’s father, were sons of Abiel.

text.NRSV: There was hard fighting against the Philistines all the days of Saul; when Saul saw any strong or valiant warrior, he took him into his service.
text.Rotherham: And the war was severe against the Philistines all the days of Saul,—and, whensoever Saul saw any mighty man, or any son of valour, he drew him unto himself.
text.KJV: 14:52 And there was sore warre against the Philistines, all the dayes of Saul: and when Saul saw any strong man, or any valiant man, he tooke him vnto him.
text.NABRE: 14:52 There was heavy fighting with the Philistines during Saul’s lifetime. Whenever Saul saw any strong or brave man, he took him into his service.

text.NRSV: Samuel said to Saul, “The Lord sent me to anoint you king over his people Israel; now therefore listen to the words of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And Samuel said unto Saul, It was, me, Yahweh sent to anoint thee to be king over his people, over Israel,—now, therefore, hearken thou to the voice of Yahweh’s words.
text.KJV: 15:1 Samuel also saide vnto Saul, The Lord sent me to annoint thee to bee king ouer his people, ouer Israel: nowe therefore hearken thou vnto the voyce of the words of the Lord.
text.NABRE: 15:1 Chapter 15 Disobedience of Saul. Samuel said to Saul: “It was I the Lord sent to anoint you king over his people Israel. Now, therefore, listen to the message of the Lord.

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord of hosts, ‘I will punish the Amalekites for what they did in opposing the Israelites when they came up out of Egypt.
text.Rotherham: Thus, saith Yahweh of hosts, I have well considered what Amalek did unto Israel—how he lay in wait for him in the way, when he came up out of Egypt.
text.KJV: 15:2 Thus saith the Lord of hosts, I remember that which Amalek did to Israel, how he laid wait for him in the way when he came vp from Egypt.
text.NABRE: 15:2 Thus says the Lord of hosts: I will punish what Amalek did to the Israelites when he barred their way as they came up from Egypt.

text.NRSV: Now go and attack Amalek, and utterly destroy all that they have; do not spare them, but kill both man and woman, child and infant, ox and sheep, camel and donkey.’”
text.Rotherham: Now, go and smite Amalek, and devote ye to destruction all that he hath, and spare him not,—but thou shalt slay both man and woman, both child and suckling, both ox and sheep, both camel and ass.
text.KJV: 15:3 Now goe, and smite Amalek, and vtterly destroy all that they haue, and spare them not; but slay both man and woman, infant and suckling, oxe and sheepe, camell and asse.
text.NABRE: 15:3 Go, now, attack Amalek, and put under the ban everything he has. Do not spare him; kill men and women, children and infants, oxen and sheep, camels and donkeys.”

text.NRSV: So Saul summoned the people, and numbered them in Telaim, two hundred thousand foot soldiers, and ten thousand soldiers of Judah.
text.Rotherham: So then Saul called together the people, and numbered them in Telaim, two hundred thousand footmen,—and ten thousand men of Judah.
text.KJV: 15:4 And Saul gathered the people together, and numbred them in Telaim, two hundred thousand footmen, and ten thousand men of Iudah.
text.NABRE: 15:4 Saul alerted the army, and at Telaim reviewed two hundred thousand foot soldiers and ten thousand men of Judah.

text.NRSV: Saul came to the city of the Amalekites and lay in wait in the valley.
text.Rotherham: And Saul came as far as the city of Amalek,—and stirred up strife in the ravine.
text.KJV: 15:5 And Saul came to a citie of Amalek, and laid waite in the valley.
text.NABRE: 15:5 Saul went to the city of Amalek and set up an ambush in the wadi.

text.NRSV: Saul said to the Kenites, “Go! Leave! Withdraw from among the Amalekites, or I will destroy you with them; for you showed kindness to all the people of Israel when they came up from Egypt.” So the Kenites withdrew from the Amalekites.
text.Rotherham: Then said Saul unto the Kenites—Go, depart, get you down out of the midst of the Amalekites, lest I destroy you with them, yet, ye, dealt in lovingkindness, with all the sons of Israel, when they came up out of Egypt. So the Kenites departed out of the midst of the Amalekites.
text.KJV: 15:6 ¶ And Saul saide vnto the Kenites, Goe, depart, get you downe from among the Amalekites, lest I destroy you with them: for yee shewed kindnesse to all the children of Israel when they came vp out of Egypt. So the Kenites departed from among the Amalekites.
text.NABRE: 15:6 He warned the Kenites: “Leave Amalek, turn aside and come down so I will not have to destroy you with them, for you were loyal to the Israelites when they came up from Egypt.” After the Kenites left,

text.NRSV: Saul defeated the Amalekites, from Havilah as far as Shur, which is east of Egypt.
text.Rotherham: And Saul smote Amalek,—from Havilah, till thou enterest Shur, which is over against Egypt.
text.KJV: 15:7 And Saul smote the Amalekites from Hauilah, vntill thou commest to Shur, that is ouer against Egypt.
text.NABRE: 15:7 Saul routed Amalek from Havilah to the approaches of Shur, on the frontier of Egypt.

text.NRSV: He took King Agag of the Amalekites alive, but utterly destroyed all the people with the edge of the sword.
text.Rotherham: And he took Agag, king of Amalek, alive,—but, all the people, devoted he to destruction at the edge of the sword.
text.KJV: 15:8 And hee tooke Agag the king of the Amalekites aliue, and vtterly destroyed all the people with the edge of the sword.
text.NABRE: 15:8 He took Agag, king of Amalek, alive, but the rest of the people he destroyed by the sword, putting them under the ban.

text.NRSV: Saul and the people spared Agag, and the best of the sheep and of the cattle and of the fatlings, and the lambs, and all that was valuable, and would not utterly destroy them; all that was despised and worthless they utterly destroyed.
text.Rotherham: But Saul and the people spared Agag, and the best of the sheep and the oxen and the lambs, also the fatlings, and all that was good, and would not devote them to destruction,—but, all the cattle that was contemptible and diseased, that, devoted they to destruction.
text.KJV: 15:9 But Saul and the people spared Agag, and the best of the sheepe, and of the oxen, and of the fatlings, and the lambes, and all that was good, and would not vtterly destroy them: but euery thing that was vile, and refuse, that they destroyed vtterly.
text.NABRE: 15:9 He and his troops spared Agag and the best of the fat sheep and oxen, and the lambs. They refused to put under the ban anything that was worthwhile, destroying only what was worthless and of no account.

text.NRSV: The word of the Lord came to Samuel:
text.Rotherham: Then came the word of Yahweh unto Samuel, saying—
text.KJV: 15:10 ¶ Then came the word of the Lord vnto Samuel, saying;
text.NABRE: 15:10 Samuel Rebukes Saul. Then the word of the Lord came to Samuel:

text.NRSV: “I regret that I made Saul king, for he has turned back from following me, and has not carried out my commands.” Samuel was angry; and he cried out to the Lord all night.
text.Rotherham: I am grieved that I made Saul to be king, for he hath turned back from following me, and, my words, hath he not established. And it was vexing to Samuel, so that he made outcry unto Yahweh, all the night.
text.KJV: 15:11 It repenteth me that I haue set vp Saul to be king: for hee is turned backe from following me, and hath not performed my commandements. And it grieued Samuel; and he cried vnto the Lord all night.
text.NABRE: 15:11 I regret having made Saul king, for he has turned from me and has not kept my command. At this Samuel grew angry and cried out to the Lord all night.

text.NRSV: Samuel rose early in the morning to meet Saul, and Samuel was told, “Saul went to Carmel, where he set up a monument for himself, and on returning he passed on down to Gilgal.”
text.Rotherham: And Samuel rose early to meet Saul, in the morning, and it was told Samuel, saying—Saul having come to Carmel, and lo! having set him up a sign, hath gone round, and passed over, and descended to Gilgal.
text.KJV: 15:12 And when Samuel rose early to meet Saul in the morning, it was tolde Samuel, saying, Saul came to Carmel, and behold, he set him vp a place, and is gone about, and passed on, and gone downe to Gilgal.
text.NABRE: 15:12 Early in the morning he went to meet Saul, but was informed that Saul had gone to Carmel, where he set up a monument in his own honor, and that on his return he had gone down to Gilgal.

text.NRSV: When Samuel came to Saul, Saul said to him, “May you be blessed by the Lord; I have carried out the command of the Lord.”
text.Rotherham: And Samuel came unto Saul,—and Saul said unto him—Blessed, be thou by Yahweh: I have established the word of Yahweh.
text.KJV: 15:13 And Samuel came to Saul, and Saul said vnto him, Blessed be thou of the Lord: I haue performed the commandement of the Lord.
text.NABRE: 15:13 When Samuel came to him, Saul greeted him: “The Lord bless you! I have kept the command of the Lord.”

text.NRSV: But Samuel said, “What then is this bleating of sheep in my ears, and the lowing of cattle that I hear?”
text.Rotherham: And Samuel said, What then is this bleating of sheep, in mine ears,—and the lowing of oxen, which I can hear?
text.KJV: 15:14 And Samuel said, What meaneth then this bleating of the sheepe in mine eares, and the lowing of the oxen which I heare?
text.NABRE: 15:14 But Samuel asked, “What, then, is this bleating of sheep that comes to my ears, the lowing of oxen that I hear?”

text.NRSV: Saul said, “They have brought them from the Amalekites; for the people spared the best of the sheep and the cattle, to sacrifice to the Lord your God; but the rest we have utterly destroyed.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Saul—From the Amalekites, have they brought them in; in that the people spared the best of the sheep, and of the oxen, to sacrifice unto Yahweh thy God,—but, the rest, have we devoted to destruction.
text.KJV: 15:15 And Saul sayde, They haue brought them from the Amalekites: for the people spared the best of the sheepe, and of the oxen, to sacrifice vnto the Lord thy God, and the rest we haue vtterly destroyed.
text.NABRE: 15:15 Saul replied: “They were brought from Amalek. The people spared the best sheep and oxen to sacrifice to the Lord, your God; but the rest we destroyed, putting them under the ban.”

text.NRSV: Then Samuel said to Saul, “Stop! I will tell you what the Lord said to me last night.” He replied, “Speak.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Samuel unto Saul, Stay, and let me tell thee, that which Yahweh hath spoken unto me, this night. And he said to him—Speak.
text.KJV: 15:16 Then Samuel sayd vnto Saul, Stay, and I will tell thee what the Lord hath said to mee this night. And he said vnto him, Say on.
text.NABRE: 15:16 Samuel said to Saul: “Stop! Let me tell you what the Lord said to me last night.” “Speak!” he replied.

text.NRSV: Samuel said, “Though you are little in your own eyes, are you not the head of the tribes of Israel? The Lord anointed you king over Israel.
text.Rotherham: And Samuel said, Was it not, when, little, thou wast in thine own eyes, that thou wast made, head of the tribes of Israel,—and that Yahweh anointed thee to be king over Israel?
text.KJV: 15:17 And Samuel said, When thou wast litle in thine owne sight, wast thou not made the Head of the tribes of Israel, and the Lord anointed thee King ouer Israel?
text.NABRE: 15:17 Samuel then said: “Though little in your own eyes, are you not chief of the tribes of Israel? The Lord anointed you king of Israel

text.NRSV: And the Lord sent you on a mission, and said, ‘Go, utterly destroy the sinners, the Amalekites, and fight against them until they are consumed.’
text.Rotherham: So then Yahweh sent thee on a journey,—and said—Go, and devastate to destruction, the sinners—the Amalekites, and make war against them, until they have consumed them.
text.KJV: 15:18 And the Lord sent thee on a iourney, and said, Goe, and vtterly destroy the sinners the Amalekites, and fight against them, vntill they be consumed.
text.NABRE: 15:18 and sent you on a mission, saying: Go and put the sinful Amalekites under a ban of destruction. Fight against them until you have exterminated them.

text.NRSV: Why then did you not obey the voice of the Lord? Why did you swoop down on the spoil, and do what was evil in the sight of the Lord?”
text.Rotherham: Wherefore, then, didst thou not hearken unto the voice of Yahweh,—but didst rush upon the spoil, and do that which was wrong in the sight of Yahweh?
text.KJV: 15:19 Wherefore then didst thou not obey the voice of the Lord, but didst flie vpon the spoile, and didst euill in the sight of the Lord ?
text.NABRE: 15:19 Why then have you disobeyed the Lord? You have pounced on the spoil, thus doing what was evil in the Lord ’s sight.”

text.NRSV: Saul said to Samuel, “I have obeyed the voice of the Lord, I have gone on the mission on which the Lord sent me, I have brought Agag the king of Amalek, and I have utterly destroyed the Amalekites.
text.Rotherham: And Saul said unto Samuel—As indeed I have hearkened unto the voice of Yahweh, and have been on the journey on which Yahweh sent me,—and have brought in Agag, king of Amalek, and, the Amalekites, have I devoted to destruction.
text.KJV: 15:20 And Saul said vnto Samuel; Yea, I haue obeyed the voice of the Lord, and haue gone the way which the Lord sent me, and haue brought Agag the king of Amalek, and haue vtterly destroyed the Amalekites.
text.NABRE: 15:20 Saul explained to Samuel: “I did indeed obey the Lord and fulfill the mission on which the Lord sent me. I have brought back Agag, the king of Amalek, and, carrying out the ban, I have destroyed the Amalekites.

text.NRSV: But from the spoil the people took sheep and cattle, the best of the things devoted to destruction, to sacrifice to the Lord your God in Gilgal.”
text.Rotherham: And the people took, of the spoil, sheep and oxen, the chief of the things so devoted,—to sacrifice unto Yahweh thy God, in Gilgal.
text.KJV: 15:21 But the people tooke of the spoile, sheepe and oxen, the chiefe of the things which should haue bene vtterly destroyed, to sacrifice vnto the Lord thy God in Gilgal.
text.NABRE: 15:21 But from the spoil the army took sheep and oxen, the best of what had been banned, to sacrifice to the Lord your God in Gilgal.”

text.NRSV: And Samuel said, “Has the Lord as great delight in burnt offerings and sacrifices, as in obeying the voice of the Lord? Surely, to obey is better than sacrifice, and to heed than the fat of rams.
text.Rotherham: Then said Samuel—Doth that which is pleasing unto Yahweh consist in ascending-offerings and sacrifices, So much as in hearkening unto the voice of Yahweh? Lo! to hearken, is, better, than, sacrifice. And, to give heed, than, the fat of rams;
text.KJV: 15:22 And Samuel saide, Hath the Lord as great delight in burnt offerings and sacrifices, as in obeying the voice of the Lord ? Behold, to obey, is better then sacrifice: and to hearken, then the fat of rammes.
text.NABRE: 15:22 But Samuel said: “Does the Lord delight in burnt offerings and sacrifices as much as in obedience to the Lord ’s command? Obedience is better than sacrifice, to listen, better than the fat of rams.

text.NRSV: For rebellion is no less a sin than divination, and stubbornness is like iniquity and idolatry. Because you have rejected the word of the Lord, he has also rejected you from being king.”
text.Rotherham: For, as the sin of divination, is, rebelliousness,—and, as transgression with household gods, is, stubbornness,—Because thou hast rejected the word of Yahweh, therefore hath he rejected thee from being king.
text.KJV: 15:23 For rebellion is as the sin of witchcraft, and stubburnnesse is as iniquitie and idolatrie: because thou hast reiected the word of the Lord, he hath also reiected thee from being king.
text.NABRE: 15:23 For a sin of divination is rebellion, and arrogance, the crime of idolatry. Because you have rejected the word of the Lord, the Lord in turn has rejected you as king.”

text.NRSV: Saul said to Samuel, “I have sinned; for I have transgressed the commandment of the Lord and your words, because I feared the people and obeyed their voice.
text.Rotherham: Then said Saul unto Samuel—I have sinned; for I have transgressed the bidding of Yahweh, and thy words,—for I feared the people, and hearkened unto their voice.
text.KJV: 15:24 ¶ And Saul said vnto Samuel, I haue sinned: for I haue transgressed the Commandement of the Lord, and thy wordes; because I feared the people, and obeyed their voice.
text.NABRE: 15:24 Rejection of Saul. Saul admitted to Samuel: “I have sinned, for I have transgressed the command of the Lord and your instructions. I feared the people and obeyed them.

text.NRSV: Now therefore, I pray, pardon my sin, and return with me, so that I may worship the Lord.”
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, I pray thee, pardon my sin,—and turn again with me, that I may bow down unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: 15:25 Now therefore, I pray thee, pardon my sinne, and turne againe with me, that I may worship the Lord.
text.NABRE: 15:25 Now forgive my sin, and return with me, that I may worship the Lord.”

text.NRSV: Samuel said to Saul, “I will not return with you; for you have rejected the word of the Lord, and the Lord has rejected you from being king over Israel.”
text.Rotherham: And Samuel said unto Saul, I will not turn again with thee,—Because thou hast rejected the word of Yahweh, therefore hath Yahweh rejected thee from being king over Israel.
text.KJV: 15:26 And Samuel said vnto Saul, I will not returne with thee: for thou hast reiected the word of the Lord, and the Lord hath reiected thee from being king ouer Israel.
text.NABRE: 15:26 But Samuel said to Saul, “I will not return with you, because you rejected the word of the Lord and the Lord has rejected you as king of Israel.”

text.NRSV: As Samuel turned to go away, Saul caught hold of the hem of his robe, and it tore.
text.Rotherham: And, when Samuel turned about to go away, then laid he hold of the skirt of his robe, and it was rent.
text.KJV: 15:27 And as Samuel turned about to goe away, he laid hold vpon the skirt of his mantle, and it rent.
text.NABRE: 15:27 As Samuel turned to go, Saul seized a loose end of his garment, and it tore off.

text.NRSV: And Samuel said to him, “The Lord has torn the kingdom of Israel from you this very day, and has given it to a neighbor of yours, who is better than you.
text.Rotherham: And Samuel said unto him, Yahweh hath rent the kingdom of Israel from off thee, to-day,—and will give it unto a neighbour of thine, who is better than thou.
text.KJV: 15:28 And Samuel said vnto him, The Lord hath rent the kingdome of Israel from thee this day, and hath giuen it to a neighbour of thine, that is better then thou.
text.NABRE: 15:28 So Samuel said to him: “The Lord has torn the kingdom of Israel from you this day, and has given it to a neighbor of yours, who is better than you.

text.NRSV: Moreover the Glory of Israel will not recant or change his mind; for he is not a mortal, that he should change his mind.”
text.Rotherham: Moreover also, the Eminence of Israel, will not lie, neither will he repent,—For no, iron of earth, is, he, to repent!
text.KJV: 15:29 And also the strength of Israel will not lie, nor repent: for he is not a man that he should repent.
text.NABRE: 15:29 The Glory of Israel neither deceives nor repents, for he is not a mortal who repents.”

text.NRSV: Then Saul said, “I have sinned; yet honor me now before the elders of my people and before Israel, and return with me, so that I may worship the Lord your God.”
text.Rotherham: Then he said: I have sinned, Now, honour me, I pray thee, before the elders of my people, and before Israel,—and turn again with me, then will I bow down unto Yahweh thy God.
text.KJV: 15:30 Then he said, I haue sinned; yet honour me now, I pray thee, before the Elders of my people, and before Israel, and turne againe with me, that I may worship the Lord thy God.
text.NABRE: 15:30 But Saul answered: “I have sinned, yet honor me now before the elders of my people and before Israel. Return with me that I may worship the Lord your God.”

text.NRSV: So Samuel turned back after Saul; and Saul worshiped the Lord.
text.Rotherham: So then Samuel turned again, after Saul—and Saul bowed down unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: 15:31 So Samuel turned againe after Saul, and Saul worshipped the Lord.
text.NABRE: 15:31 And so Samuel returned with him, and Saul worshiped the Lord.

text.NRSV: Then Samuel said, “Bring Agag king of the Amalekites here to me.” And Agag came to him haltingly. Agag said, “Surely this is the bitterness of death.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Samuel—Bring ye near unto me—Agag, king of Amalek, And Agag came unto him, in fetters. Then said Agag, Surely, terrible, is the bitterness of death!
text.KJV: 15:32 ¶ Then said Samuel, Bring you hither to me Agag the king of the Amalekites: and Agag came vnto him delicately. And Agag said, Surely the bitternesse of death is past.
text.NABRE: 15:32 Samuel Executes Agag. Afterward Samuel commanded, “Bring Agag, king of Amalek, to me.” Agag came to him struggling and saying, “So it is bitter death!”

text.NRSV: But Samuel said, “As your sword has made women childless, so your mother shall be childless among women.” And Samuel hewed Agag in pieces before the Lord in Gilgal.
text.Rotherham: And Samuel said, As women have been made childless by thy sword, So, childless among women, shall be, thine own mother. And Samuel cut Agag asunder before Yahweh, in Gilgal.
text.KJV: 15:33 And Samuel said, As thy sword hath made women childlesse, so shall thy mother bee childlesse among women. And Samuel hewed Agag in pieces before the Lord in Gilgal.
text.NABRE: 15:33 And Samuel said, “As your sword has made women childless, so shall your mother be childless among women.” Then he cut Agag to pieces before the Lord in Gilgal.

text.NRSV: Then Samuel went to Ramah; and Saul went up to his house in Gibeah of Saul.
text.Rotherham: Then Samuel departed unto Ramah,—but, Saul, went up unto his own house, at Gibeah of Saul.
text.KJV: 15:34 ¶ Then Samuel went to Ramah, and Saul went vp to his house to Gibeah of Saul.
text.NABRE: 15:34 Samuel departed for Ramah, while Saul went up to his home in Gibeah of Saul.

text.NRSV: Samuel did not see Saul again until the day of his death, but Samuel grieved over Saul. And the Lord was sorry that he had made Saul king over Israel.
text.Rotherham: And Samuel did no more see Saul, until the day of his death, for Samuel pined for Saul,—but, Yahweh, was grieved that he had made Saul king over Israel.
text.KJV: 15:35 And Samuel came no more to see Saul vntill the day of his death: neuerthelesse, Samuel mourned for Saul: and the Lord repented that he had made Saul king ouer Israel.
text.NABRE: 15:35 Never again, as long as he lived, did Samuel see Saul. Yet he grieved over Saul, because the Lord repented that he had made him king of Israel.

text.NRSV: The Lord said to Samuel, “How long will you grieve over Saul? I have rejected him from being king over Israel. Fill your horn with oil and set out; I will send you to Jesse the Bethlehemite, for I have provided for myself a king among his sons.”
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said unto Samuel—How long, art thou going to pine for Saul, seeing that, I, have rejected him from being king over Israel? Fill thy horn with oil, and come! let me send thee unto Jesse the Bethlehemite, for I have provided me, among his sons, a king.
text.KJV: 16:1 And the Lord said vnto Samuel, How long wilt thou mourne for Saul, seeing I haue reiected him from reigning ouer Israel? Fill thine horne with oile, and goe, I will send thee to Iesse the Bethlehemite: for I haue prouided mee a King among his sonnes.
text.NABRE: 16:1 Chapter 16 Samuel Is Sent to Bethlehem. The Lord said to Samuel: How long will you grieve for Saul, whom I have rejected as king of Israel? Fill your horn with oil, and be on your way. I am sending you to Jesse of Bethlehem, for from among his sons I have decided on a king.

text.NRSV: Samuel said, “How can I go? If Saul hears of it, he will kill me.” And the Lord said, “Take a heifer with you, and say, ‘I have come to sacrifice to the Lord.’”
text.Rotherham: And Samuel said, How can I go? As soon as Saul heareth, he will slay me. Then said Yahweh, A heifer, take thou with thee, and say, To sacrifice unto Yahweh, am I come.
text.KJV: 16:2 And Samuel said, How can I goe? if Saul heare it, he will kill mee. And the Lord said, Take an heifer with thee, and say, I am come to sacrifice to the Lord.
text.NABRE: 16:2 But Samuel replied: “How can I go? Saul will hear of it and kill me.” To this the Lord answered: Take a heifer along and say, “I have come to sacrifice to the Lord.”

text.NRSV: Invite Jesse to the sacrifice, and I will show you what you shall do; and you shall anoint for me the one whom I name to you.”
text.Rotherham: Then shall thou bid Jesse to the sacrifice,—when, I, will let thee know what thou must do, so shalt thou anoint for me him whom I shall name unto thee.
text.KJV: 16:3 And call Iesse to the sacrifice, and I will shew thee what thou shalt doe: and thou shalt anoynt vnto mee him whom I name vnto thee.
text.NABRE: 16:3 Invite Jesse to the sacrifice, and I myself will tell you what to do; you are to anoint for me the one I point out to you.

text.NRSV: Samuel did what the Lord commanded, and came to Bethlehem. The elders of the city came to meet him trembling, and said, “Do you come peaceably?”
text.Rotherham: And Samuel did that which Yahweh had spoken, and went to Bethlehem. And the elders of the city came trembling to meet him, and one said—Peaceably, comest thou?
text.KJV: 16:4 And Samuel did that which the Lord spake, and came to Bethlehem: and the elders of the towne trembled at his comming, and said, Commest thou peaceably?
text.NABRE: 16:4 Samuel Anoints David. Samuel did as the Lord had commanded him. When he entered Bethlehem, the elders of the city came trembling to meet him and asked, “Is your visit peaceful, O seer?”

text.NRSV: He said, “Peaceably; I have come to sacrifice to the Lord; sanctify yourselves and come with me to the sacrifice.” And he sanctified Jesse and his sons and invited them to the sacrifice.
text.Rotherham: And he said—Peaceably: to sacrifice unto Yahweh, am I come. Hallow yourselves, and come with me to the sacrifice. And he hallowed Jesse and his sons, and bade them to the sacrifice.
text.KJV: 16:5 And hee said, Peaceably: I am come to sacrifice vnto the Lord: sanctifie your selues, and come with me to the sacrifice: and he sanctified Iesse, and his sonnes, and called them to the sacrifice.
text.NABRE: 16:5 He replied: “Yes! I have come to sacrifice to the Lord. So purify yourselves and celebrate with me today.” He also had Jesse and his sons purify themselves and invited them to the sacrifice.

text.NRSV: When they came, he looked on Eliab and thought, “Surely the Lord’s anointed is now before the Lord.”
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when they entered, that he looked upon Eliab,—and said to himself, Surely, before Yahweh, is his Anointed.
text.KJV: 16:6 ¶ And it came to passe when they were come, that he looked on Eliab, and said, Surely the Lords anointed is before him.
text.NABRE: 16:6 As they came, he looked at Eliab and thought, “Surely the anointed is here before the Lord.”

text.NRSV: But the Lord said to Samuel, “Do not look on his appearance or on the height of his stature, because I have rejected him; for the Lord does not see as mortals see; they look on the outward appearance, but the Lord looks on the heart.”
text.Rotherham: But Yahweh said unto Samuel—Do not regard his countenance, or the height of his stature, for I have rejected him,—for it is not what man looketh to but what God looketh to. For, man, looketh to the outward appearance, but, Yahweh, looketh to the heart.
text.KJV: 16:7 But the Lord said vnto Samuel, Looke not on his countenance, or on the height of his stature, because I haue refused him: for the Lord seeth not, as man seeth; For man looketh on the outward appearance, but the Lord looketh on the heart.
text.NABRE: 16:7 But the Lord said to Samuel: Do not judge from his appearance or from his lofty stature, because I have rejected him. God does not see as a mortal, who sees the appearance. The Lord looks into the heart.

text.NRSV: Then Jesse called Abinadab, and made him pass before Samuel. He said, “Neither has the Lord chosen this one.”
text.Rotherham: Then Jesse called Abinadab, and made him pass before Samuel,—and he said, Neither of this one, hath Yahweh made choice.
text.KJV: 16:8 Then Iesse called Abinadab, and made him passe before Samuel: and he said, Neither hath the Lord chosen this.
text.NABRE: 16:8 Then Jesse called Abinadab and presented him before Samuel, who said, “The Lord has not chosen him.”

text.NRSV: Then Jesse made Shammah pass by. And he said, “Neither has the Lord chosen this one.”
text.Rotherham: Then Jesse made Shammah pass by,—and he said—Nor of this one, hath Yahweh made choice.
text.KJV: 16:9 Then Iesse made Shammah to passe by: and he said, Neither hath the Lord chosen this.
text.NABRE: 16:9 Next Jesse presented Shammah, but Samuel said, “The Lord has not chosen this one either.”

text.NRSV: Jesse made seven of his sons pass before Samuel, and Samuel said to Jesse, “The Lord has not chosen any of these.”
text.Rotherham: So Jesse made seven of his sons pass before Samuel,—and Samuel said unto Jesse, Yahweh hath not made choice of these.
text.KJV: 16:10 Againe Iesse made seuen of his sonnes to passe before Samuel; and Samuel said vnto Iesse, The Lord hath not chosen these.
text.NABRE: 16:10 In the same way Jesse presented seven sons before Samuel, but Samuel said to Jesse, “The Lord has not chosen any one of these.”

text.NRSV: Samuel said to Jesse, “Are all your sons here?” And he said, “There remains yet the youngest, but he is keeping the sheep.” And Samuel said to Jesse, “Send and bring him; for we will not sit down until he comes here.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Samuel unto Jesse—Are these all the young men? And he said—There yet remaineth, the youngest, but lo! he is tending the sheep. Then said Samuel unto Jesse—Do, send, and summon him, for we cannot sit round, until he cometh in hither.
text.KJV: 16:11 And Samuel saide vnto Iesse, Are here all thy children? And he said, There remaineth yet the yongest, and behold, he keepeth the sheepe. And Samuel said vnto Iesse, Send, and fetch him: for we will not sit downe, till hee come hither.
text.NABRE: 16:11 Then Samuel asked Jesse, “Are these all the sons you have?” Jesse replied, “There is still the youngest, but he is tending the sheep.” Samuel said to Jesse, “Send for him; we will not sit down to eat until he arrives here.”

text.NRSV: He sent and brought him in. Now he was ruddy, and had beautiful eyes, and was handsome. The Lord said, “Rise and anoint him; for this is the one.”
text.Rotherham: So he sent, and brought him in. Now, he, was ruddy, a stripling with handsome eyes, and noble mien. Then said Yahweh—Rise—anoint him, for, this, is, he.
text.KJV: 16:12 And he sent, and brought him in: now he was ruddy, and withal of a beautifull countenance, and goodly to looke to: And the Lord said, Arise, anoint him: for this is he.
text.NABRE: 16:12 Jesse had the young man brought to them. He was ruddy, a youth with beautiful eyes, and good looking. The Lord said: There—anoint him, for this is the one!

text.NRSV: Then Samuel took the horn of oil, and anointed him in the presence of his brothers; and the spirit of the Lord came mightily upon David from that day forward. Samuel then set out and went to Ramah.
text.Rotherham: And Samuel took the horn of oil, and anointed him in the midst of his brethren. And the Spirit of Yahweh came mightily upon David, from that day forward. Then arose Samuel, and went his way unto Ramah.
text.KJV: 16:13 Then Samuel tooke the horne of oile, and annointed him in the midst of his brethren: and the Spirit of the Lord came vpon Dauid, from that day forward: So Samuel rose vp and went to Ramah.
text.NABRE: 16:13 Then Samuel, with the horn of oil in hand, anointed him in the midst of his brothers, and from that day on, the spirit of the Lord rushed upon David. Then Samuel set out for Ramah.

text.NRSV: Now the spirit of the Lord departed from Saul, and an evil spirit from the Lord tormented him.
text.Rotherham: But, the spirit of Yahweh, departed from Saul,—and there terrified him a sad spirit, from Yahweh.
text.KJV: 16:14 ¶ But the spirit of the Lord departed from Saul, and an euil spirit from the Lord troubled him.
text.NABRE: 16:14 David Wins Saul’s Approval. The spirit of the Lord had departed from Saul, and he was tormented by an evil spirit from the Lord.

text.NRSV: And Saul’s servants said to him, “See now, an evil spirit from God is tormenting you.
text.Rotherham: Then said the servants of Saul unto him,—Lo! we pray thee, a sad superhuman spirit, doth terrify thee:
text.KJV: 16:15 And Sauls seruants said vnto him, Behold now, an euill spirit from God troubleth thee.
text.NABRE: 16:15 So the servants of Saul said to him: “Look! An evil spirit from God is tormenting you.

text.NRSV: Let our lord now command the servants who attend you to look for someone who is skillful in playing the lyre; and when the evil spirit from God is upon you, he will play it, and you will feel better.”
text.Rotherham: Pray let our lord bid thy servants before thee, seek out a man, skilled in playing on the lyre,—so shall it be, when a sad superhuman spirit cometh on thee, then shall he play with his hand, and thou shalt be joyful.
text.KJV: 16:16 Let our lord now command thy seruants which are before thee, to seeke out a man, who is a cunning player on an harpe: and it shall come to passe when the euill spirit from God is vpon thee, that hee shall play with his hand, and thou shalt be well.
text.NABRE: 16:16 If your lordship will order it, we, your servants here attending to you, will look for a man skilled in playing the harp. When the evil spirit from God comes upon you, he will play and you will feel better.”

text.NRSV: So Saul said to his servants, “Provide for me someone who can play well, and bring him to me.”
text.Rotherham: And Saul said unto his servants,—I pray you, look out for me a man that excelleth in playing, and bring him in unto me.
text.KJV: 16:17 And Saul said vnto his seruants, Prouide mee now a man, that can play well, and bring him to me.
text.NABRE: 16:17 Saul then told his servants, “Find me a good harpist and bring him to me.”

text.NRSV: One of the young men answered, “I have seen a son of Jesse the Bethlehemite who is skillful in playing, a man of valor, a warrior, prudent in speech, and a man of good presence; and the Lord is with him.”
text.Rotherham: Then responded one of the young men and said—Lo! I have seen—a son of Jesse, the Bethlehemite, skilled in playing, and a mighty man of valour, and a man of war, and of intelligent speech, and a handsome man, and, Yahweh, is with him.
text.KJV: 16:18 Then answered one of the seruants, and said, Behold, I haue seene a sonne of Iesse the Bethlehemite, that is cunning in playing, and a mighty valiant man, and a man of warre, and prudent in matters, and a comely person, and the Lord is with him.
text.NABRE: 16:18 One of the servants spoke up: “I have observed that a son of Jesse of Bethlehem is a skillful harpist. He is also a brave warrior, an able speaker, and a handsome young man. The Lord is certainly with him.”

text.NRSV: So Saul sent messengers to Jesse, and said, “Send me your son David who is with the sheep.”
text.Rotherham: So Saul sent messengers unto Jesse,—and said, Send, unto me—David thy son, who is with the sheep.
text.KJV: 16:19 ¶ Wherefore Saul sent messengers vnto Iesse, and said, Send me Dauid thy sonne, which is with the sheepe.
text.NABRE: 16:19 David Made Armor-Bearer. Accordingly, Saul dispatched messengers to ask Jesse to send him his son David, who was with the flock.

text.NRSV: Jesse took a donkey loaded with bread, a skin of wine, and a kid, and sent them by his son David to Saul.
text.Rotherham: Then took Jesse an ass laden with bread, and a skin of wine, and one kid,—and sent by the hand of David his son, unto Saul.
text.KJV: 16:20 And Iesse tooke an asse laden with bread, and a bottle of wine, and a kid, and sent them by Dauid his sonne vnto Saul.
text.NABRE: 16:20 Then Jesse took five loaves of bread, a skin of wine, and a young goat, and sent them to Saul with his son David.

text.NRSV: And David came to Saul, and entered his service. Saul loved him greatly, and he became his armor-bearer.
text.Rotherham: And David came in unto Saul,—and stood before him,—and he loved him greatly, and he became his armour-bearer.
text.KJV: 16:21 And Dauid came to Saul, and stood before him: and hee loued him greatly, and hee became his armour bearer.
text.NABRE: 16:21 Thus David came to Saul and entered his service. Saul became very fond of him and made him his armor-bearer.

text.NRSV: Saul sent to Jesse, saying, “Let David remain in my service, for he has found favor in my sight.”
text.Rotherham: And Saul sent unto Jesse, saying,—I pray thee, let David stand before me, for he hath found favour in mine eyes.
text.KJV: 16:22 And Saul sent to Iesse, saying, Let Dauid, I pray thee, stand before me: for hee hath found fauour in my sight.
text.NABRE: 16:22 Saul sent Jesse the message, “Let David stay in my service, for he meets with my approval.”

text.NRSV: And whenever the evil spirit from God came upon Saul, David took the lyre and played it with his hand, and Saul would be relieved and feel better, and the evil spirit would depart from him.
text.Rotherham: And so it used to be, when a superhuman spirit came unto Saul, then would David take the lyre, and play with his hand,—and Saul would be refreshed, and be joyful, and, the sad spirit, would depart from him.
text.KJV: 16:23 And it came to passe, when the euill spirit from God was vpon Saul, that Dauid tooke an harpe, and played with his hand: So Saul was refreshed, and was well, and the euill spirit departed from him.
text.NABRE: 16:23 Whenever the spirit from God came upon Saul, David would take the harp and play, and Saul would be relieved and feel better, for the evil spirit would leave him.

text.NRSV: Now the Philistines gathered their armies for battle; they were gathered at Socoh, which belongs to Judah, and encamped between Socoh and Azekah, in Ephes-dammim.
text.Rotherham: And the Philistines gathered together their hosts, unto battle, and they were gathered together unto Socoh, which belongeth unto Judah,—and they encamped between Socoh and Azekah in Ephes-dammim;
text.KJV: 17:1 Now the Philistines gathered together their armies to battell, and were gathered together at Shochoh, which belongeth to Iudah, and pitched betweene Shochoh and Azekah, in Ephes-Dammim.
text.NABRE: 17:1 Chapter 17 The Challenge of Goliath. The Philistines rallied their forces for battle at Socoh in Judah and camped between Socoh and Azekah at Ephes-dammim.

text.NRSV: Saul and the Israelites gathered and encamped in the valley of Elah, and formed ranks against the Philistines.
text.Rotherham: and, Saul and the men of Israel, gathered themselves together, and encamped in the vale of Elah, and set the army in array against the Philistines.
text.KJV: 17:2 And Saul and the men of Israel were gathered together, and pitched by the valley of Elah, and set the battell in aray against the Philistines.
text.NABRE: 17:2 Saul and the Israelites rallied and camped in the valley of the Elah, drawing up their battle line to meet the Philistines.

text.NRSV: The Philistines stood on the mountain on the one side, and Israel stood on the mountain on the other side, with a valley between them.
text.Rotherham: And, the Philistines, were standing near the hill on one side, and, the Israelites, were standing near the hill on the other side,—while, the valley, was between them.
text.KJV: 17:3 And the Philistines stood on a mountaine on the one side, and Israel stood on a mountaine on the other side: and there was a valley betweene them.
text.NABRE: 17:3 The Philistines were stationed on one hill and the Israelites on an opposite hill, with a valley between them.

text.NRSV: And there came out from the camp of the Philistines a champion named Goliath, of Gath, whose height was six cubits and a span.
text.Rotherham: And there came forth a champion out of the camp of the Philistines, Goliath, his name, from Gath,—his height, six cubits and a span;
text.KJV: 17:4 ¶ And there went out a champion out of the campe of the Philistines, named Goliath of Gath: whose height was sixe cubites and a span.
text.NABRE: 17:4 A champion named Goliath of Gath came out from the Philistine camp; he was six cubits and a span tall.

text.NRSV: He had a helmet of bronze on his head, and he was armed with a coat of mail; the weight of the coat was five thousand shekels of bronze.
text.Rotherham: with a helmet of bronze on his head, and, with a scaly coat of mail, was he clad,—the weight of the coat, being five thousand shekels of bronze;
text.KJV: 17:5 And he had an helmet of brasse vpon his head, and he was armed with a coate of male: and the weight of the coat was fiue thousand shekels of brasse.
text.NABRE: 17:5 He had a bronze helmet on his head and wore a bronze breastplate of scale armor weighing five thousand shekels,

text.NRSV: He had greaves of bronze on his legs and a javelin of bronze slung between his shoulders.
text.Rotherham: and, greaves of bronze, on his feet,—and, a javelin of bronze, between his shoulders;
text.KJV: 17:6 And he had greaues of brasse vpon his legs, and a target of brasse betweene his shoulders.
text.NABRE: 17:6 bronze greaves, and had a bronze scimitar slung from his shoulders.

text.NRSV: The shaft of his spear was like a weaver’s beam, and his spear’s head weighed six hundred shekels of iron; and his shield-bearer went before him.
text.Rotherham: and, the shaft of his spear, was like a weaver’s beam, and, the flashing head of his spear, was six hundred shekels of iron,—and, his shield-bearer, was coming on before him.
text.KJV: 17:7 And the staffe of his speare was like a weauers beame, and his speares head weighed sixe hundred shekels of yron: and one bearing a shield, went before him.
text.NABRE: 17:7 The shaft of his javelin was like a weaver’s beam, and its iron head weighed six hundred shekels. His shield-bearer went ahead of him.

text.NRSV: He stood and shouted to the ranks of Israel, “Why have you come out to draw up for battle? Am I not a Philistine, and are you not servants of Saul? Choose a man for yourselves, and let him come down to me.
text.Rotherham: And he took his stand, and cried unto the ranks of Israel, and said unto them, Wherefore should ye come out, to set in array for battle? Am not, I, a Philistine, while, ye, are servants unto Saul? Choose you a man, and let him come down unto me:
text.KJV: 17:8 And hee stood and cried vnto the armies of Israel, and said vnto them, Why are yee come out to set your battell in aray? am not I a Philistine, and you seruants to Saul? chuse you a man for you, and let him come downe to me.
text.NABRE: 17:8 He stood and shouted to the ranks of Israel: “Why come out in battle formation? I am a Philistine, and you are Saul’s servants. Choose one of your men, and have him come down to me.

text.NRSV: If he is able to fight with me and kill me, then we will be your servants; but if I prevail against him and kill him, then you shall be our servants and serve us.”
text.Rotherham: If he prevail in fighting with me, and smite me, then will we become your servants,—but, if, I, prevail over him, and smite him, then shall ye become our servants, and serve, us.
text.KJV: 17:9 If he be able to fight with mee, and to kill me, then will we be your seruants: but if I preuaile against him, and kill him, then shall yee be our seruants, and serue vs.
text.NABRE: 17:9 If he beats me in combat and kills me, we will be your vassals; but if I beat him and kill him, you shall be our vassals and serve us.”

text.NRSV: And the Philistine said, “Today I defy the ranks of Israel! Give me a man, that we may fight together.”
text.Rotherham: And the Philistine said—I, reproach the ranks of Israel, this day,—give me a man, that we may fight together.
text.KJV: 17:10 And the Philistine said, I defie the armies of Israel this day; giue me a man, that we may fight together.
text.NABRE: 17:10 The Philistine continued: “I defy the ranks of Israel today. Give me a man and let us fight together.”

text.NRSV: When Saul and all Israel heard these words of the Philistine, they were dismayed and greatly afraid.
text.Rotherham: When Saul and all Israel heard these words of the Philistine, they were dismayed, and feared exceedingly.
text.KJV: 17:11 When Saul and all Israel heard those words of the Philistine, they were dismayed, and greatly afraid.
text.NABRE: 17:11 When Saul and all Israel heard this challenge of the Philistine, they were stunned and terrified. David Comes to the Camp.

text.NRSV: Now David was the son of an Ephrathite of Bethlehem in Judah, named Jesse, who had eight sons. In the days of Saul the man was already old and advanced in years.
text.Rotherham: Now, David, was the son of that Ephrathite of Bethlehem-judah, whose, name, was Jesse, and, who, had eight sons,—and, the man, in the days of Saul was old, advanced in years.
text.KJV: 17:12 ¶ Now Dauid was the sonne of that Ephrathite of Bethlehem Iudah, whose name was Iesse, and hee had eight sonnes: and the man went among men for an old man in the dayes of Saul.
text.NABRE: 17:12 David was the son of an Ephrathite named Jesse from Bethlehem in Judah who had eight sons. In the days of Saul Jesse was old and well on in years.

text.NRSV: The three eldest sons of Jesse had followed Saul to the battle; the names of his three sons who went to the battle were Eliab the firstborn, and next to him Abinadab, and the third Shammah.
text.Rotherham: And the three eldest sons of Jesse had followed Saul to the battle,—and, the names of his three sons who went into the battle, were Eliab the firstborn, and, the next to him, Abinadab, and, the third, Shammah.
text.KJV: 17:13 And the three eldest sonnes of Iesse went, and followed Saul to the battell: and the names of his three sonnes that went to the battell, were, Eliab the first borne, and next vnto him, Abinadab, and the third, Shammah.
text.NABRE: 17:13 The three oldest sons of Jesse had followed Saul to war; the names of these three sons who had gone off to war were Eliab the firstborn; Abinadab the second; and Shammah the third.

text.NRSV: David was the youngest; the three eldest followed Saul,
text.Rotherham: But, as for David, he, was the youngest,—and, the three eldest, followed Saul;
text.KJV: 17:14 And Dauid was the yongest: and the three eldest followed Saul.
text.NABRE: 17:14 David was the youngest. While the three oldest had joined Saul,

text.NRSV: but David went back and forth from Saul to feed his father’s sheep at Bethlehem.
text.Rotherham: whereas, David, kept going and returning from Saul,—to tend his father’s sheep at Bethlehem.
text.KJV: 17:15 But Dauid went, and returned from Saul, to feed his fathers sheepe at Bethlehem.
text.NABRE: 17:15 David would come and go from Saul’s presence to tend his father’s sheep at Bethlehem.

text.NRSV: For forty days the Philistine came forward and took his stand, morning and evening.
text.Rotherham: And the Philistine drew near morning and evening,—and presented himself forty days.
text.KJV: 17:16 And the Philistine drewe neere, morning and euening, and presented himselfe forty dayes.
text.NABRE: 17:16 Meanwhile the Philistine came forward and took his stand morning and evening for forty days.

text.NRSV: Jesse said to his son David, “Take for your brothers an ephah of this parched grain and these ten loaves, and carry them quickly to the camp to your brothers;
text.Rotherham: And Jesse said unto David his son, Take, I pray thee, unto thy brethren—this ephah of parched corn, and these ten loaves,—and run to the camp unto thy brethren;
text.KJV: 17:17 And Iesse said vnto Dauid his sonne, Take now for thy brethren an ephah of this parched corne, and these ten loaues, and run to the campe to thy brethren.
text.NABRE: 17:17 Now Jesse said to his son David: “Take this ephah of roasted grain and these ten loaves for your brothers, and bring them quickly to your brothers in the camp.

text.NRSV: also take these ten cheeses to the commander of their thousand. See how your brothers fare, and bring some token from them.”
text.Rotherham: also these ten slices of soft cheese, shalt thou take to the captain of their thousand,—and, as for thy brethren, give good heed to their welfare, their pledge, also shalt thou receive.
text.KJV: 17:18 And carie these ten cheeses vnto the Captaine of their thousand, and looke how thy brethren fare, and take their pledge.
text.NABRE: 17:18 Also take these ten cheeses for the field officer. Greet your brothers and bring home some token from them.

text.NRSV: Now Saul, and they, and all the men of Israel, were in the valley of Elah, fighting with the Philistines.
text.Rotherham: Now, Saul, and they, and all the men of Israel, were in the vale of Elah, ready to fight with the Philistines.
text.KJV: 17:19 Now Saul, and they, and all the men of Israel were in the valley of Elah, fighting with the Philistines.
text.NABRE: 17:19 Saul and your brothers, together with all Israel, are at war with the Philistines in the valley of the Elah.”

text.NRSV: David rose early in the morning, left the sheep with a keeper, took the provisions, and went as Jesse had commanded him. He came to the encampment as the army was going forth to the battle line, shouting the war cry.
text.Rotherham: So then David rose up early in the morning, and entrusted the sheep to a keeper, and took up [the provisions] and went his way, as Jesse had commanded him,—and came into the circular rampart, as, the force, was going forth into the ranks, and shouted for the fight.
text.KJV: 17:20 ¶ And Dauid rose vp earely in the morning, and left the sheepe with a keeper, and tooke, and went, as Iesse had commanded him; and he came to the trench, as the host was going forth to the fight, and shouted for the battell.
text.NABRE: 17:20 Early the next morning, having left the flock with a shepherd, David packed up and set out, as Jesse had commanded him. He reached the barricade of the camp just as the army, on their way to the battleground, were shouting their battle cry.

text.NRSV: Israel and the Philistines drew up for battle, army against army.
text.Rotherham: So Israel and the Philistines set in array for battle, army against army.
text.KJV: 17:21 For Israel and the Philistines had put the battel in aray, army against armie.
text.NABRE: 17:21 The Israelites and the Philistines drew up opposite each other in battle array.

text.NRSV: David left the things in charge of the keeper of the baggage, ran to the ranks, and went and greeted his brothers.
text.Rotherham: Then David entrusted the provisions that were upon him to the care of the keeper of the stores, and ran unto the ranks,—and came and asked for the welfare of his brethren.
text.KJV: 17:22 And Dauid left his cariage in the hand of the keeper of the cariage, and ranne into the armie, and came and saluted his brethren.
text.NABRE: 17:22 David entrusted what he had brought to the keeper of the baggage and hastened to the battle line, where he greeted his brothers.

text.NRSV: As he talked with them, the champion, the Philistine of Gath, Goliath by name, came up out of the ranks of the Philistines, and spoke the same words as before. And David heard him.
text.Rotherham: Now, as he was speaking with them, lo! the champion coming up, Goliath the Philistine, by name, of Gath, [coming up] out of the ranks of the Philistines, and he spake according to these words,—and David heard [them] .
text.KJV: 17:23 And as he talked with them, behold, there came vp the champion (the Philistine of Gath, Goliath by name) out of the armies of the Philistines, and spake according to the same words: and Dauid heard them.
text.NABRE: 17:23 While he was talking with them, the Philistine champion, by name Goliath of Gath, came up from the ranks of the Philistines and spoke as before, and David listened.

text.NRSV: All the Israelites, when they saw the man, fled from him and were very much afraid.
text.Rotherham: Now, all the men of Israel, when they saw the man, fled from before him, and feared exceedingly.
text.KJV: 17:24 And all the men of Israel, when they saw the man, fled from him, and were sore afraid.
text.NABRE: 17:24 When the Israelites saw the man, they all retreated before him, terrified.

text.NRSV: The Israelites said, “Have you seen this man who has come up? Surely he has come up to defy Israel. The king will greatly enrich the man who kills him, and will give him his daughter and make his family free in Israel.”
text.Rotherham: And the men of Israel said—Have ye seen this man that is coming up? For, to reproach Israel, is he coming. So then it shall be, that, the man that shall smite him, the same, will the king enrich with great riches, and, his own daughter, will give him, and, his father’s house, will he make free in Israel.
text.KJV: 17:25 And the men of Israel said, Haue yee seene this man that is come vp? Surely to defie Israel is he come vp: and it shall be that the man who killeth him, the king wil enrich him with great riches, and will giue him his daughter, and make his fathers house free in Israel.
text.NABRE: 17:25 The Israelites had been saying: “Do you see this man coming up? He comes up to insult Israel. The king will make whoever kills him a very wealthy man. He will give his daughter to him and declare his father’s family exempt from taxes in Israel.”

text.NRSV: David said to the men who stood by him, “What shall be done for the man who kills this Philistine, and takes away the reproach from Israel? For who is this uncircumcised Philistine that he should defy the armies of the living God?”
text.Rotherham: Then spake David unto the men that were standing by him, saying, What shall be done to the man that shall smite yonder Philistine, and so shall take away reproach from off Israel? For who is this uncircumcised Philistine that he hath reproached the ranks of a Living God?
text.KJV: 17:26 And Dauid spake to the men that stood by him, saying; What shall bee done to the man that killeth this Philistine; and taketh away the reproch from Israel? for who is this vncircumcised Philistine, that he should defie the armies of the liuing God?
text.NABRE: 17:26 David now said to the men standing near him: “How will the man who kills this Philistine and frees Israel from disgrace be rewarded? Who is this uncircumcised Philistine that he should insult the armies of the living God?”

text.NRSV: The people answered him in the same way, “So shall it be done for the man who kills him.”
text.Rotherham: And the people answered him, according to this word saying,—So, shall it be done to the man that shall smite him.
text.KJV: 17:27 And the people answered him after this maner, saying, So shall it be done to the man that killeth him.
text.NABRE: 17:27 They repeated the same words to him and said, “That is how the man who kills him will be rewarded.”

text.NRSV: His eldest brother Eliab heard him talking to the men; and Eliab’s anger was kindled against David. He said, “Why have you come down? With whom have you left those few sheep in the wilderness? I know your presumption and the evil of your heart; for you have come down just to see the battle.”
text.Rotherham: Now Eliab his eldest brother heard when he spake unto the men,—then was kindled the anger of Eliab against David, and he said—Wherefore is it that thou hast come down? and to whom hast thou entrusted those few sheep in the wilderness? I, know thy pride, and the foolishness of thy heart, for, to see the battle, hast thou come down.
text.KJV: 17:28 ¶ And Eliab his eldest brother heard when he spake vnto the men, and Eliabs anger was kindled against Dauid, and he said, Why camest thou down hither? and with whom hast thou left those few sheepe in the wildernesse? I know thy pride, and the naughtinesse of thine heart; for thou art come downe, that thou mightest see the battell.
text.NABRE: 17:28 When Eliab, his oldest brother, heard him speaking with the men, he grew angry with David and said: “Why did you come down? With whom have you left those sheep in the wilderness? I know your arrogance and dishonest heart. You came down to enjoy the battle!”

text.NRSV: David said, “What have I done now? It was only a question.”
text.Rotherham: And David said, What have I done, now? Was there not, a cause?
text.KJV: 17:29 And Dauid saide, What haue I now done? Is there not a cause?
text.NABRE: 17:29 David protested, “What have I done now? I was only talking.”

text.NRSV: He turned away from him toward another and spoke in the same way; and the people answered him again as before.
text.Rotherham: Then turned he from him unto another, and said—according to this word,—and the people returned him an answer, like the first.
text.KJV: 17:30 ¶ And hee turned from him towards another, and spake after the same maner: and the people answered him againe after the former maner.
text.NABRE: 17:30 He turned from him to another and asked the same question; and everyone gave him the same answer as before.

text.NRSV: When the words that David spoke were heard, they repeated them before Saul; and he sent for him.
text.Rotherham: And the words which David spake were overheard,—and, when they told them before Saul, he summoned him.
text.KJV: 17:31 And when the words were heard which Dauid spake, they rehearsed them before Saul: and he sent for him.
text.NABRE: 17:31 The words that David had spoken were overheard and reported to Saul, who sent for him.

text.NRSV: David said to Saul, “Let no one’s heart fail because of him; your servant will go and fight with this Philistine.”
text.Rotherham: And David said unto Saul, Let not the heart of my lord fail, because of him,—thy servant, will go, and fight with this Philistine.
text.KJV: 17:32 ¶ And Dauid said to Saul, Let no mans heart faile, because of him: thy seruant will goe and fight with this Philistine.
text.NABRE: 17:32 David Challenges Goliath. Then David spoke to Saul: “My lord should not lose heart. Let your servant go and fight this Philistine.”

text.NRSV: Saul said to David, “You are not able to go against this Philistine to fight with him; for you are just a boy, and he has been a warrior from his youth.”
text.Rotherham: And Saul said unto David—Thou art not able to go against this Philistine, to fight with him,—for, a youth, art, thou, but, he, a man of war, from his youth.
text.KJV: 17:33 And Saul said to Dauid, Thou art not able to goe against this Philistine, to fight with him: for thou art but a youth, and he a man of warre from his youth.
text.NABRE: 17:33 But Saul answered David, “You cannot go up against this Philistine and fight with him, for you are only a youth, while he has been a warrior from his youth.”

text.NRSV: But David said to Saul, “Your servant used to keep sheep for his father; and whenever a lion or a bear came, and took a lamb from the flock,
text.Rotherham: Then said David unto Saul, Thy servant used to tend his father’s sheep,—and there would come a lion, or a bear, and carry off a lamb out of the flock;
text.KJV: 17:34 And Dauid said vnto Saul, Thy seruant kept his fathers sheepe, and there came a Lyon, and a Beare, and tooke a lambe out of the flocke:
text.NABRE: 17:34 Then David told Saul: “Your servant used to tend his father’s sheep, and whenever a lion or bear came to carry off a sheep from the flock,

text.NRSV: I went after it and struck it down, rescuing the lamb from its mouth; and if it turned against me, I would catch it by the jaw, strike it down, and kill it.
text.Rotherham: and I would go out after him, and smite him, and rescue it out of his mouth,—and, if he rose against me, I would catch him by his beard, and smite him, and slay him.
text.KJV: 17:35 And I went out after him, and smote him, and deliuered it out of his mouth: and when he arose against me, I caught him by his beard, and smote him, and slew him.
text.NABRE: 17:35 I would chase after it, attack it, and snatch the prey from its mouth. If it attacked me, I would seize it by the throat, strike it, and kill it.

text.NRSV: Your servant has killed both lions and bears; and this uncircumcised Philistine shall be like one of them, since he has defied the armies of the living God.”
text.Rotherham: Thy servant could slay, either a lion or a bear,—and, this uncircumcised Philistine, shall become, as one of them, for he hath reproached the ranks of a Living God.
text.KJV: 17:36 Thy seruant slew both the Lyon and the Beare: and this vncircumcised Philistine shall be as one of them, seeing he hath defied the armies of the liuing God.
text.NABRE: 17:36 Your servant has killed both a lion and a bear. This uncircumcised Philistine will be as one of them, because he has insulted the armies of the living God.”

text.NRSV: David said, “The Lord, who saved me from the paw of the lion and from the paw of the bear, will save me from the hand of this Philistine.” So Saul said to David, “Go, and may the Lord be with you!”
text.Rotherham: And David said, Yahweh, who hath rescued me out of the power of the lion, and out of the power of the bear, he, will rescue us out of the hand of this Philistine. Then said Saul unto David—Go! and, Yahweh, will be with thee.
text.KJV: 17:37 Dauid saide moreouer, The Lord that deliuered me out of the paw of the Lyon, and out of the pawe of the Beare, he will deliuer me out of the hand of this Philistine. And Saul said vnto Dauid, Goe, and the Lord be with thee.
text.NABRE: 17:37 David continued: “The same Lord who delivered me from the claws of the lion and the bear will deliver me from the hand of this Philistine.” Saul answered David, “Go! the Lord will be with you.”

text.NRSV: Saul clothed David with his armor; he put a bronze helmet on his head and clothed him with a coat of mail.
text.Rotherham: And Saul clad David with his own military coat, and set a helmet of bronze upon his head,—and clad him with a coat of mail.
text.KJV: 17:38 ¶ And Saul armed Dauid with his armour, and hee put an helmet of brasse vpon his head, also he armed him with a coat of male.
text.NABRE: 17:38 Preparation for the Encounter. Then Saul dressed David in his own tunic, putting a bronze helmet on his head and arming him with a coat of mail.

text.NRSV: David strapped Saul’s sword over the armor, and he tried in vain to walk, for he was not used to them. Then David said to Saul, “I cannot walk with these; for I am not used to them.” So David removed them.
text.Rotherham: And David girded his sword above his military coat, but was reluctant to go, for he had not proved them,—so David said unto Saul—I cannot go in these, for I have not proved them. And David put them off him.
text.KJV: 17:39 And Dauid girded his sword vpon his armour, and he assayed to goe, for he had not proued it: and Dauid said vnto Saul, I cannot goe with these: for I haue not proued them. And Dauid put them off him.
text.NABRE: 17:39 David also fastened Saul’s sword over the tunic. He walked with difficulty, however, since he had never worn armor before. He said to Saul, “I cannot go in these, because I am not used to them.” So he took them off.

text.NRSV: Then he took his staff in his hand, and chose five smooth stones from the wadi, and put them in his shepherd’s bag, in the pouch; his sling was in his hand, and he drew near to the Philistine.
text.Rotherham: Then took he his stick in his hand, and chose him five smooth stones out of the torrent-bed, and put them in the shepherd’s-pouch which he had—even in the wallet, and had his sling in his hand,—and so drew near unto the Philistine.
text.KJV: 17:40 And hee tooke his staffe in his hand, and chose him fiue smoothe stones out of the brooke, and put them in a shepheards bag which he had, euen in a scrip, and his sling was in his hande, and he drew neere to the Philistine.
text.NABRE: 17:40 Then, staff in hand, David selected five smooth stones from the wadi and put them in the pocket of his shepherd’s bag. With his sling in hand, he approached the Philistine.

text.NRSV: The Philistine came on and drew near to David, with his shield-bearer in front of him.
text.Rotherham: And the Philistine came on and on, and drew near unto David,—and the man carrying the shield was before him.
text.KJV: 17:41 And the Philistine came on and drew neere vnto Dauid, and the man that bare the shield, went before him.
text.NABRE: 17:41 David’s Victory. With his shield-bearer marching before him, the Philistine advanced closer and closer to David.

text.NRSV: When the Philistine looked, and saw David, he disdained him; for he was only a youth, ruddy and handsome in appearance.
text.Rotherham: And, when the Philistine looked about, and saw David, he disdained him,—for he was a youth, and ruddy, a stripling of fair countenance.
text.KJV: 17:42 And when the Philistine looked about, and saw Dauid, hee disdained him: for he was but a youth, and ruddy, and of a faire countenance.
text.NABRE: 17:42 When he sized David up and saw that he was youthful, ruddy, and handsome in appearance, he began to deride him.

text.NRSV: The Philistine said to David, “Am I a dog, that you come to me with sticks?” And the Philistine cursed David by his gods.
text.Rotherham: Then said the Philistine unto David, A dog, am, I, that thou art coming unto me, with sticks? And the Philistine cursed David, by his god.
text.KJV: 17:43 And the Philistine said vnto Dauid, Am I a dog, that thou commest to me with staues? and the Philistine cursed Dauid by his gods.
text.NABRE: 17:43 He said to David, “Am I a dog that you come against me with a staff?” Then the Philistine cursed David by his gods

text.NRSV: The Philistine said to David, “Come to me, and I will give your flesh to the birds of the air and to the wild animals of the field.”
text.Rotherham: And the Philistine said unto David,—Come, unto me, that I may give thy flesh to the birds of heaven, and to the beasts of the field.
text.KJV: 17:44 And the Philistine said to Dauid, Come to me, and I will giue thy flesh vnto the foules of the aire, and to the beasts of the field.
text.NABRE: 17:44 and said to him, “Come here to me, and I will feed your flesh to the birds of the air and the beasts of the field.”

text.NRSV: But David said to the Philistine, “You come to me with sword and spear and javelin; but I come to you in the name of the Lord of hosts, the God of the armies of Israel, whom you have defied.
text.Rotherham: Then said David unto the Philistine—Thou, art coming unto me with sword, and with spear, and with javelin,—but, I, am coming unto thee in the name of Yahweh of hosts, God of the ranks of Israel which thou hast reproached.
text.KJV: 17:45 Then said Dauid to the Philistine, Thou commest to mee with a sword, and with a speare, and with a shield: but I come to thee in the Name of the Lord of hostes, the God of the armies of Israel, whom thou hast defied.
text.NABRE: 17:45 David answered him: “You come against me with sword and spear and scimitar, but I come against you in the name of the Lord of hosts, the God of the armies of Israel whom you have insulted.

text.NRSV: This very day the Lord will deliver you into my hand, and I will strike you down and cut off your head; and I will give the dead bodies of the Philistine army this day to the birds of the air and to the wild animals of the earth, so that all the earth may know that there is a God in Israel,
text.Rotherham: This day, will Yahweh deliver thee into my hand, and I will smite thee, and take thy head from off thee, and will give thy dead body and the dead bodies of the host of Philistines, this day, unto the birds of heaven, and unto the wild beasts of the earth,—that all the earth may know that Israel hath a God;
text.KJV: 17:46 This day wil the Lord deliuer thee into mine hand, and I will smite thee, and take thine head from thee, and I wil giue the carkeises of the host of the Philistines this day vnto the foules of the aire, and to the wild beasts of the earth, that all the earth may know that there is a God in Israel.
text.NABRE: 17:46 Today the Lord shall deliver you into my hand; I will strike you down and cut off your head. This very day I will feed your dead body and the dead bodies of the Philistine army to the birds of the air and the beasts of the field; thus the whole land shall learn that Israel has a God.

text.NRSV: and that all this assembly may know that it is not by sword or spear that the Lord saves; for the battle is the Lord’s and he will give you into our hand.”
text.Rotherham: and that all this gathered host may know that, not with sword and with spear, doth Yahweh save,—for, unto Yahweh, belongeth the battle, and he will deliver you into our hand.
text.KJV: 17:47 And all this assembly shal know that the Lord saueth not with sword & speare (for the battell is the Lords ) and he will giue you into our hands.
text.NABRE: 17:47 All this multitude, too, shall learn that it is not by sword or spear that the Lord saves. For the battle belongs to the Lord, who shall deliver you into our hands.”

text.NRSV: When the Philistine drew nearer to meet David, David ran quickly toward the battle line to meet the Philistine.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when the Philistine arose, and came and drew near to meet David, that David hasted, and ran toward the ranks to meet the Philistine.
text.KJV: 17:48 And it came to passe when the Philistine arose, and came, and drewe nigh to meet Dauid, that Dauid hasted, and ran toward the armie to meete the Philistine.
text.NABRE: 17:48 The Philistine then moved to meet David at close quarters, while David ran quickly toward the battle line to meet the Philistine.

text.NRSV: David put his hand in his bag, took a stone, slung it, and struck the Philistine on his forehead; the stone sank into his forehead, and he fell face down on the ground.
text.Rotherham: And David thrust his hand into his pouch, and took from thence a stone, and slang it, and smote the Philistine, in his forehead,—that the stone sank into his forehead, and he fell upon his face, to the earth.
text.KJV: 17:49 And Dauid put his hande in his bag, and tooke thence a stone, and slang it, & smote the Philistine in his forehead, that the stone sunke into his forehead, and he fell vpon his face to the earth.
text.NABRE: 17:49 David put his hand into the bag and took out a stone, hurled it with the sling, and struck the Philistine on the forehead. The stone embedded itself in his brow, and he fell on his face to the ground.

text.NRSV: So David prevailed over the Philistine with a sling and a stone, struck down the Philistine and killed him; there was no sword in David’s hand.
text.Rotherham: Thus David prevailed over the Philistine, with the sling and with the stone, and smote the Philistine, and slew him,—but, sword, was there none in the hand of David.
text.KJV: 17:50 So Dauid preuailed ouer the Philistine with a sling and with a stone, and smote the Philistine, and slew him, but there was no sword in the hande of Dauid.
text.NABRE: 17:50 Thus David triumphed over the Philistine with sling and stone; he struck the Philistine dead, and did it without a sword in his hand.

text.NRSV: Then David ran and stood over the Philistine; he grasped his sword, and drew it out of its sheath, and killed him; then he cut off his head with it. When the Philistines saw that their champion was dead, they fled.
text.Rotherham: So David ran, and stood by the Philistine, and took his sword and drew it out of the sheath thereof, and slew him, and cut off, therewith, his head,—and, when the Philistines saw that their hero was slain, they fled.
text.KJV: 17:51 Therefore Dauid ran and stood vpon the Philistine, and tooke his sword, and drewe it out of the sheath thereof, and slew him, and cut off his head therewith. And when the Philistines sawe their champion was dead, they fled.
text.NABRE: 17:51 Then David ran and stood over him; with the Philistine’s own sword which he drew from its sheath he killed him, and cut off his head. Flight of the Philistines. When the Philistines saw that their hero was dead, they fled.

text.NRSV: The troops of Israel and Judah rose up with a shout and pursued the Philistines as far as Gath and the gates of Ekron, so that the wounded Philistines fell on the road from Shaaraim as far as Gath and Ekron.
text.Rotherham: Then arose the men of Israel and Judah, and shouted, and pursued the Philistines, as far as the entrance into Gath, and as far as the gates of Ekron,—and the slain of the Philistines fell in the way to the two gates, even as far as Gath and as far as Ekron.
text.KJV: 17:52 And the men of Israel, and of Iudah arose, and shouted, and pursued the Philistines, vntill thou come to the valley, and to the gates of Ekron: and the wounded of the Philistines fell downe by the way to Shaaraim, euen vnto Gath, and vnto Ekron.
text.NABRE: 17:52 Then the men of Israel and Judah sprang up with a battle cry and pursued them to the approaches of Gath and to the gates of Ekron, and Philistines fell wounded along the road from Shaaraim as far as Gath and Ekron.

text.NRSV: The Israelites returned from chasing the Philistines and plundered their camp.
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Israel returned from hotly pursuing the Philistines,—and plundered their camps.
text.KJV: 17:53 And the children of Israel returned from chasing after the Philistines, and they spoiled their tents.
text.NABRE: 17:53 When they returned from their pursuit of the Philistines, the Israelites looted their camp.

text.NRSV: David took the head of the Philistine and brought it to Jerusalem; but he put his armor in his tent.
text.Rotherham: And David took the head of the Philistine, and brought it into Jerusalem,—but, his armour, put he into his tent.
text.KJV: 17:54 And Dauid tooke the head of the Philistine, and brought it to Ierusalem, but he put his armour in his tent.
text.NABRE: 17:54 David took the head of the Philistine and brought it to Jerusalem; but he kept Goliath’s armor in his own tent.

text.NRSV: When Saul saw David go out against the Philistine, he said to Abner, the commander of the army, “Abner, whose son is this young man?” Abner said, “As your soul lives, O king, I do not know.”
text.Rotherham: And, when Saul saw David going forth to meet the Philistine, he said unto Abner, prince of the host, Whose son is the young man, Abner? And Abner said, By the life of thy soul, O king! I know not.
text.KJV: 17:55 ¶ And when Saul sawe Dauid goe forth against the Philistine, he sayd vnto Abner the captaine of the hoste, Abner, whose sonne is this youth? And Abner said, As thy soule liueth, O king, I cannot tell.
text.NABRE: 17:55 David Presented to Saul. As Saul watched David go out to meet the Philistine, he asked his general Abner, “Abner, whose son is that young man?” Abner replied, “On your life, O king, I have no idea.”

text.NRSV: The king said, “Inquire whose son the stripling is.”
text.Rotherham: Then said the king,—Ask, thou, whose son the stripling is?
text.KJV: 17:56 And the king said, Enquire thou whose sonne the stripling is.
text.NABRE: 17:56 And the king said, “Find out whose son the lad is.”

text.NRSV: On David’s return from killing the Philistine, Abner took him and brought him before Saul, with the head of the Philistine in his hand.
text.Rotherham: And, when David returned from smiting the Philistine, Abner took him, and brought him before Saul,—with the head of the Philistine in his hand.
text.KJV: 17:57 And as Dauid returned from the slaughter of the Philistine, Abner tooke him, & brought him before Saul, with the head of the Philistine in his hand.
text.NABRE: 17:57 So when David returned from slaying the Philistine, Abner escorted him into Saul’s presence. David was still holding the Philistine’s head.

text.NRSV: Saul said to him, “Whose son are you, young man?” David answered, “I am the son of your servant Jesse the Bethlehemite.”
